name: nextjs-15-patterns
description: Next.js 15 App Router patterns and best practices.
capabilities: Generate server component code with async/await patterns
Create server action implementations with Zod validation
Implement parallel and sequential data fetching with Suspense
Build route handlers and API endpoints with NextResponse
Add metadata generation and dynamic route patterns
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing TypeScript code examples for Next.js 15 patterns. Static scanner flagged markdown code blocks and YAML syntax as false positives. No executable code, scripts, or runtime network calls. Safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: This skill provides best practices and code patterns for Next.js 15 App Router development, covering server components, server actions, data fetching strategies, and modern React 19 patterns.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify project files directly
Does not install dependencies or configure build tools
Does not interact with databases or external services
Provides patterns and examples rather than complete solutions

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers modernizing legacy Next.js pages
useCases.title: Migrate to Server Components
useCases.description: Refactor client-side components to server components where possible, reducing bundle size and improving performance.

useCases.title: Implement Server Actions
useCases.description: Replace API routes with server actions for form handling and data mutations, simplifying the codebase.

useCases.targetUser: Performance-focused developers optimizing Core Web Vitals
useCases.title: Optimize Data Fetching
useCases.description: Apply parallel fetching, caching strategies, and Suspense streaming for faster page loads.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Server Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a server component in Next.js 15 that fetches data from a database.

promptTemplates.title: Server Action with Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a server action with Zod schema validation that handles form submission and revalidates the cache.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a validated form with server actions

promptTemplates.title: Parallel Data Fetching
promptTemplates.prompt: Write code for fetching multiple data sources in parallel using Promise.all with Suspense fallbacks.

promptTemplates.title: Full Page Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete Next.js 15 page with server components, loading states, error handling, and metadata generation.

bestPractices: Prefer server components by default and only use 'use client' when client-side interactivity is required
Fetch data directly in server components rather than using API routes for internal data access
Use Zod for server action validation and return typed error responses
antiPatterns: Avoid fetching data in client components with useEffect; use server components instead
Do not use API routes for mutations when server actions provide a simpler alternative
Avoid blocking rendering with sequential fetches when data can be loaded in parallel

faq.question: What is the default component type in Next.js 15?
faq.answer: Server components are the default in Next.js 15 App Router. Components are server-rendered unless marked with 'use client'.

faq.question: When should I use server actions versus API routes?
faq.answer: Use server actions for form submissions and data mutations. Use API routes only when you need to expose endpoints to external clients.

faq.question: How does data caching work in Next.js 15?
faq.answer: Fetch requests are cached by default. Use cache: 'no-store' for dynamic data, or next: { revalidate: 60 } for time-based revalidation.

faq.question: What is Suspense streaming?
faq.answer: Suspense allows parts of the page to stream in progressively while other parts are still loading, improving perceived performance.

faq.question: Can I use React hooks in server components?
faq.answer: No, hooks like useState and useEffect only work in client components. Server components render on the server without React state.

faq.question: How do I handle errors in server components?
faq.answer: Create an error.tsx file with 'use client' to handle errors. Use notFound() for 404 cases and throw errors to trigger the error boundary.
